source.title: Fatal Vessel Leak, Vapor Cloud Explosion, and Fire at Yenkin-Majestic Resin Plant

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Vapor Cloud Explosion and Fire
event.incident_type: Vessel Leak, Vapor Cloud Explosion, and Fire
actions_taken: Employee A attempted to turn off agitator but could not reach emergency stop button due to vapor obscuring visibility
Employee A evacuated the kettle room approximately 80 seconds after release began
Employee B assisted Employee A in evacuation
More than 100 firefighters responded including hazmat teams
Firefighters rescued two injured employees trapped in collapsed building
Ohio EPA responded and contained firefighting water runoff entering Alum Creek
Yenkin-Majestic demolished the severely damaged resin plant over 19-month period
event.summary: On April 8, 2021, at approximately 12:02 a.m., a mixture of flammable naphtha solvent vapors and resin liquid became pressurized and released through the seal of a closed manway on Kettle 3 at the Yenkin-Majestic OPC Polymers resin plant in Columbus, Ohio. The release occurred after the kettle operator turned on the agitator, which caused rapid vaporization of solvent that had been added to the hot resin while the agitator was unexpectedly off. The flammable vapor cloud spread throughout the enclosed building and formed both inside and outside. Approximately two minutes later, at 12:04 a.m., the vapor cloud ignited, causing an explosion and large fire. One employee was fatally injured from thermal injuries and inhalation of combustion products. Eight other employees were transported to hospitals with injuries including third-degree burns and limb fractures, with one requiring leg amputation. The incident caused over $90 million in property damage.
recommendations: Update mechanical integrity procedures for all process vessels in highly hazardous chemicals service, including pressure vessels not exceeding 15 psig, to adopt alteration guidance in API 510 or National Board Inspection Code Part 3

Assess and document applicable design, construction, and alteration standards for all pressure vessels in highly hazardous chemicals service, adopting PIP VESLP001 Low-Pressure Welded Vessel Specification at minimum
Implement program to assess pressure vessels against updated RAGAGEP at least once every five years
Develop and implement written procedures to document and maintain records of all inspections, alterations, and maintenance/repairs on all pressure vessels in highly hazardous chemicals service
Demonstrate use of prevention through design using hierarchy of controls in future resin plant designs, prioritizing inherently safer design and engineering controls
Identify and document all equipment that could release flammable materials and install LEL detectors in accordance with CCPS guidelines
Ensure detection of hazardous conditions automatically triggers both visual and audible alarms to alert plant personnel
Develop and implement employee training on actions to take when alarms are activated, such as prompt evacuation
Develop and implement requirements for personnel to wear flame-resistant uniforms in all operating areas that process flammable chemicals
Update employee training material to include requirement for and purpose of PPE use
API to develop specific design, construction, and alteration guidance for low-pressure process vessels in flammable and other highly hazardous chemicals service not exceeding 15 psig
ASME to assist API in developing design, construction, and alteration guidance for low-pressure vessels in flammable and other highly hazardous chemicals service not exceeding 15 psig
key_phrases: safeguard selection and hierarchy of controls

hazards.name: Flammable solvent vapors in enclosed space
Pressurized vessel containing hot resin and solvent
Inadequate mechanical integrity of pressure vessel manway
Stratified liquid layers in reactor vessel

threats.name: Manway seal failure due to inadequate design pressure
Rapid pressure increase from solvent vaporization upon agitator restart
Agitator shutdown without operator awareness or alarm notification
Solvent addition to kettle with agitator off, creating stratified layers
Rupture disk failure to burst at design pressure
Flammable vapor cloud formation and migration throughout building
Lack of audible alarm to alert personnel of flammable vapor release

consequences.name: Loss of Containment - release of flammable solvent vapor and hot resin
Fatal injury - one employee fatally injured from thermal injuries and inhalation of combustion products
Multiple serious injuries - eight employees transported to hospitals with burns and fractures, one requiring leg amputation
Environmental impact - firefighting water runoff into Alum Creek

controls.name: Pressure vessel design and construction standards
controls.lod_basis: Design phase control to ensure vessel can withstand operating pressures

supporting_text: Kettle 3 normally operated at or near atmospheric pressure but was known to potentially build pressure up to 15 pounds per square inch gauge (psig) during some upset conditions
After the new manway was added, Yenkin-Majestic performed a leak check up to 4 psig and allowed Kettle 3 to continue operating for approximately three months until the incident
On the day of the incident, Kettle 3's new manway did not withstand more than 9 psig of pressure during a process upset and leaked hot resin and flammable solvent vapor into the facility
Yenkin-Majestic did not follow basic quality assurance practices, such as adequately pressure testing equipment after alterations

controls.name: Pressure testing after vessel alterations
controls.lod_basis: Quality assurance practice to ensure vessel can withstand design pressure

supporting_text: After the new manway was added, Yenkin-Majestic performed a leak check up to 4 psig and allowed Kettle 3 to continue operating for approximately three months until the incident
Yenkin-Majestic did not follow basic quality assurance practices, such as adequately pressure testing equipment after alterations, which led to this release

controls.name: Agitator status monitoring and alarm
controls.lod_basis: Prevent operator from adding solvent when agitator is off

supporting_text: At 10:22 p.m. the Kettle 3 agitator unexpectedly shut down

Employee A returned a few minutes later, at 10:25 p.m., and did not realize that the agitator had shut down while he was away; no alarms were active at the time to indicate a process abnormality, either

Yenkin-Majestic had not installed or configured interlocks to prevent solvent addition to the kettle when the agitator was off during normal batch operations and relied upon computer panel status indicators, that were not equipped with alarms, to communicate the agitator status to the operator

controls.name: Interlock to prevent solvent addition when agitator is off
controls.barrier_role: Prevent solvent addition during agitator shutdown
controls.lod_basis: Prevent stratified liquid layers that lead to rapid vaporization

supporting_text: Yenkin-Majestic had not installed or configured interlocks to prevent solvent addition to the kettle when the agitator was off during normal batch operations
The system design allowed an operator to add solvent to the hot-resin-filled kettle while, unknown to the operator, the agitator (which was supposed to mix the liquids in the kettle) was off

controls.name: High-pressure alarm at 2 psig
controls.lod_basis: Early warning of pressure increase to allow operator intervention

supporting_text: The kettle was configured with a high-pressure alarm at 2 pounds per square inch gauge (psig), and an automatic high-pressure trip at 4 psig
During the April 8, 2021, incident, the high-pressure alarm and trip activated after Kettle 3's pressure began to rise

controls.name: Automatic high-pressure trip at 4 psig with emergency cooling interlock
controls.barrier_role: Automatically shut down furnace and activate cooling water
controls.lod_basis: Automatic response to reduce temperature and pressure

supporting_text: The high-pressure trip activated the emergency cooling safety interlock, which was designed to shut down the furnace and turn on cooling water to the coils to aid in reducing the temperature and pressure inside the kettle
The pressure continued to increase even after triggering the kettle's high-pressure alarm and safety interlock

controls.name: Rupture disk set at 13.5-15 psig burst pressure
controls.barrier_role: Relieve pressure by directing contents to catch tank
controls.lod_basis: Final pressure relief mechanism to prevent vessel rupture

supporting_text: The Kettle 3 emergency pressure-relief system also included one 8-inch rupture disk, used to protect the vessel from high-pressure conditions, which Yenkin-Majestic documented as having a burst pressure between 13.5 and 15 psig
During the April 8, 2021, incident, the rupture disk did not burst
At this peak pressure, Kettle 3's pressure was above the rupture disk's marked pressure setting but still within its burst tolerance range, and it did not burst to relieve Kettle 3's pressure
The tag on Kettle 3's recovered rupture disk post-incident indicated a marked burst pressure of 16.5 psig

controls.name: Flammable gas detectors in furnace room
controls.lod_basis: Early detection of flammable vapor to enable response

supporting_text: Multiple flammable gas detectors inside the furnace room on the first floor started detecting an increasing concentration of flammable vapors during the release
The flammable gas detectors triggered automatic furnace shutdowns; however, they were not configured to sound an audible alarm

The gas monitors installed within the Yenkin-Majestic facility detected the flammable solvent release approximately one minute after the release began and sent an email to an offsite employee, but the monitors did not sound audible alarms to warn employees of the hazard

controls.name: Audible alarm system for flammable gas detection
controls.lod_basis: Immediate notification to enable timely evacuation

supporting_text: Yenkin-Majestic did not effectively utilize flammable gas detection systems and associated alarms to audibly notify onsite personnel of a hazardous gas release and the need to evacuate
The monitors did not sound audible alarms to warn employees of the hazard
Yenkin-Majestic did not have adequate mitigative safeguards to minimize the consequences of the incident

controls.name: Employee training on flammable vapor hazards and response
controls.barrier_role: Enable hazard recognition and appropriate response
controls.lod_basis: Knowledge to recognize hazard and initiate evacuation

supporting_text: Yenkin-Majestic did not specifically train its employees to recognize and respond to the presence of a flammable solvent vapor cloud and its associated hazards
During the incident, this overall lack of hazard recognition led to some personnel approaching the hazardous gas to investigate the release instead of initiating a plant-wide evacuation
At 12:03:30 a.m., an employee saw the white vapor from the outside of the building and began running into the resin plant to investigate what he thought was a steam leak

controls.name: Fire alarm system activation for evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Immediate notification to enable timely evacuation

supporting_text: No one used the resin plant's intercom communication system to call for an evacuation or otherwise warn workers of the imminent danger
Post-incident, Yenkin-Majestic also confirmed that the fire alarm system had not been activated to warn workers to evacuate prior to the explosion
Employees working at or near the resin plant that night told CSB investigators that they did not hear any alarms or other warnings before the explosion
Yenkin-Majestic allowed resin plant operators to wear cotton short-sleeved shirts while working in proximity to flammable materials unless they were performing specific tasks

Had the resin plant employees been required to wear flame-resistant personal protective equipment (PPE), multiple employee burn injuries resulting from the explosion and fire may have been reduced or prevented
Four of the injured employees were burned by the fire, two of whom received third-degree burns

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
